title: Hahnia intermedia
intro: Hahnia intermedia, also known as the false maidenhair fern or Hahnia fern, is a species of delicate, non-vascular plants belonging to the family Hahniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hahnia intermedia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been incorporated into herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are used to promote respiratory health and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Hahnia intermedia is a species of spider in the family Hahniidae. This classification places Hahnia intermedia within the order Araneomorphae. The family Hahniidae is a group of spiders known as 'ant-mimicking spiders' due to their appearance and behavior. Hahnia intermedia is also classified within the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, and class Arachnida. At the species level, Hahnia intermedia is likely classified as a member of the genus Hahnia.
